Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lynn R. Fiorentino, Esq., of NIXON PEABODY (415-984-8412), appearing for defendant / moving party Ford Motor Company
Eric Policastro, Esq., of BARON & BUDD, P.C. (800-222-2766), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Lloyd Kendrick, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and off calendar as to issues 1, 2, and 3 (breach of warranty, false representation and intentional tort). While California law applies to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. As to issues 1-3, plaintiffs represent that they are dismissing those causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES, U.S.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Toyota Motor Sales, USA, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (breach of warranty, false representation and intentional tort) and denied as to issue 4 (punitive damages). As to issues 1-3, plaintiffs represent that they are dismissing those causes of action. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Defendant's separate statement does not include any assertedly undisputed fact that defendant propounded contention discovery regarding plaintiffs' request for punitive damages. Counsel for plaintiffs is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lysle Kapp, Esq., of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Nissan North American, Inc.
Eric Policastro, Esq., of BARON & BUDD, P.C. (214-521-3605/800-222-2766), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Lloyd Kendrick, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Nissan North America, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain: 1) evidence that: Mrs. Kendrick was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant and 2) clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Regarding Mrs. Kendrick's exposure, Mr. Kendrick's deposition testimony and defendant's responses to plaintiffs' special interrogatories from this case preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Regarding punitive damages, defendant's separate statement does not include any assertedly undisputed fact that defendant propounded contention discovery (other than requests for documents) regarding plaintiffs' request for punitive damages. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Argued and tentative ruling is modified as follows: PLAINTIFFS LLOYD KENDRICK, JAMES KENDRICK, GARY RHEINOR, RONALD RHEINOR, CONNIE WATSON'S motion to add American Honda Motor Co., Inc. as a defendant is granted on the following conditions: the newly added defendant may file a summary motion on or before August 3, 2011 with plaintiffs' opposition due by August 17, 2011 and any reply papers due by August 24, 2011 with a hearing date of September 1, 2011 at 9:30 am in Department 220. The trial date is continued to September 12, 2011 at 11:15 am in Department 220. If American Honda believes after making diligent efforts to be ready for trial, but yet needs further time, it may file a motion to continue. Percipient witness discovery closes as to all defendants other than American Honda, triggered to the previously set trial date of August 1. As to American Honda, percipient discovery remains open up to August 26. Expert discovery for all defendants is triggered off of the new trial date of September 12. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
